title: Racosperma stellaticeps
taxonomy: Racosperma stellaticeps is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Western Australia and grows in the southwestern region. The plant has been classified into different ranks within its taxonomy. It is also known to be a part of the genus Acacia, which has been reclassified as Racosperma in some classifications. However, more recent studies suggest that Racosperma stellaticeps belongs to the genus Acacia.
uses: Racosperma stellaticeps is known to have some potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine.
